device_probe.root_cause: freedreno Gallium pipe-loader requires /dev/dri/renderD128 (DRM render node). Termux has no DRM subsystem. KGSL device /dev/kgsl-3d0 is accessible but pipe_loader_drm_probe() only scans /dev/dri/renderD* nodes.
device_probe.kgsl_driver_descriptor_note: kgsl_driver_descriptor is DRM_DRIVER_DESCRIPTOR_ALIAS(msm, kgsl) — it still expects a DRM fd, not a raw KGSL fd
device_probe.turnip_comparison: Turnip Vulkan works because tu_knl_kgsl.cc opens /dev/kgsl-3d0 directly, bypassing DRM entirely

conclusion.reason: Rusticl platform initializes (OpenCL 3.1) but cannot enumerate GPU device without DRM render node. Fundamental architectural limitation of Gallium pipe-loader on non-DRM Android/Termux. Would require custom pipe-loader KGSL path (not upstream).
conclusion.possible_fix: Implement pipe_loader_kgsl_probe() that opens /dev/kgsl-3d0 and creates fd_device via kgsl_device_new() — significant upstream-incompatible work
